lesson.menu_desc: Start and end a call, and take a message -- who called, and what to say -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 14 -- On the Phone -- Calls & Taking a Message
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 14 -- On the Phone

listenings.text: Good morning, EGEA Saneamento, this is Daniel speaking. I am sorry, but Karen is not available right now. She is in a meeting until eleven. Would you like to leave a message, or would you like to call back later? If it is urgent, I can reach her by text. Let me take your name and number, and I will tell her you called. She usually checks her voicemail at lunch, so she will call you back this afternoon. Thank you for calling, and have a good day.

Hi Percival, this is Karen. I am sorry I missed your call. I was on another line and the connection was bad, so I had to hang up. I got your message from Daniel. You wanted to talk about the supplier report. I am available after three o'clock today. Could you call me back then, or send me a quick text? If you cannot reach me, please leave a voicemail and I will return your call first thing tomorrow. Thank you, and talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival calls the office and asks to speak to Karen. Then, Daniel says Karen is not available right now. After that, Daniel offers to take a message. Later, Percival leaves a clear message with his name and number. Finally, Daniel says he will tell Karen, and she will call back this afternoon.
